title: chimaphila umbellata subsp. occidentalis
intro: Chimaphila umbellata subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western prince's pine, is a subspecies of the twinflower plant, which belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Western prince's pine include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying and processing its leaves and stems to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
